The ScienceBase body states that the release contains network-adjusted relative- and absolute-gravity survey results from 2019 to 2024 in and near the Santa Cruz River channel in Tucson; relative surveys used a ZLS Burris relative-gravity meter; absolute surveys used a Micro-g LaCoste A-10; solid Earth tides and ocean loading were removed; drift was removed using repeated base-station measurements; vertical gradients correlated instrument heights; and GSadjust least-squares network adjustment combined relative and absolute observations. The purpose text says data were collected with Tucson Water to evaluate aquifer-storage change; the 2020-05-13 and 2020-05-27 surveys capture effects of channel modification and excavation, and data are not corrected for soil-moisture variation. Downloaded https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/file/get/62a3804bd34ec53d2770bd9b on 2026-07-13; ZIP size in retrieval was 10,655 bytes and contained Heritage_AdjustedGravity_2019-2024.csv 12,228 bytes md5 fab9fb28d3917611141e1af2a4a0cfd5, VersionHistory.txt 1,571 bytes md5 69175bbdb7321cb134ee9aebb8105a46, and Heritage_AdjustedGravity_2019-2024.xml 30,058 bytes md5 2976940c0b40f60cf65f357aef008900, all matching ScienceBase inventory. CSV parsing found 72 nonblank station rows, 72 unique station names, and no duplicate station names. CSV coordinate ranges are latitude 32.19818-32.25559 and longitude -110.99762 to -110.95496; elevation has 70 numeric values from 700.0 to 843.91 and two NA values. The CSV has ten survey-date gravity columns: 2019-08-12, 2019-11-13, 2020-03-02, 2020-05-13, 2020-05-27, 2020-11-06, 2021-03-05, 2022-03-14, 2023-03-27, and 2024-03-05. Treating -999 as the source no-data sentinel, valid gravity counts by date are 18, 19, 62, 10, 21, 21, 62, 59, 59, and 62, with 3 stations valid across all ten dates. Valid gravity values range from 979000000.0 to 979254478.1; the low value is station 1555 on 2023-03-27 represented as 9.79E+08, so it is recorded as a source precision anomaly rather than corrected. FGDC XML lists no access constraints and use constraints stating Creative Commons CC0 1.0 Universal/public domain with metadata-use advice. VersionHistory.txt says revision 1.2 adds 2023-03-27_g, 2023-03-27_sd, 2024-03-05_g, and 2024-03-05_sd. Related primary publication DOI 10.3133/sir20235115 was identified from ScienceBase webLinks.
notes: Accepted in v115 as a distinct USGS ScienceBase repeat-microgravity data release for the Santa Cruz River channel in Tucson. It is distinct from existing Tucson Basin/Avra Valley, South Houghton, Phoenix, Anza, and Rio Rancho rows by DOI, ScienceBase item ID, title, project area, and revision history. The CSV uses -999 as a no-data sentinel and includes two NA elevations; valid-count statistics exclude -999. Station 1555 has 2023-03-27_g stored as 9.79E+08, a lower-precision source value recorded without correction. No HTML dashboard was generated.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供亚利桑那州 Tucson 市 Santa Cruz River 河道及邻近区域 2019-2024 年重复微重力调查的网络平差结果。数据包包含 72 个站点的 CSV、版本历史和 FGDC 元数据，重点服务于人工回灌与含水层储量变化监测。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides network-adjusted repeat microgravity results from 2019 to 2024 along and near the Santa Cruz River channel in Tucson, Arizona. The ScienceBase package includes CSV data, version history, and FGDC metadata for 72 stations, supporting artificial-recharge and aquifer-storage change studies.

ScienceBase body states that the dataset contains network-adjusted relative- and absolute-gravity survey results; relative surveys used two ZLS Corporation Burris relative-gravity meters; absolute surveys used a Micro-g LaCoste A-10; solid Earth tides and ocean loading were removed; instrument drift was removed using repeated base-station measurements; vertical gradients correlated measuring heights; and GSadjust least-squares network adjustment combined relative-gravity differences and absolute-gravity data. Purpose text states that data were collected to evaluate aquifer-storage change in the Tucson Active Management Area and may be converted to free-standing-water thickness using a horizontal infinite-slab approximation; data are not corrected for soil-moisture variation. The CSV MD5 matches the ScienceBase inventory; the ScienceBase inventory reports XML MD5 1c296c4d30bf7c01620448f86b9bcf7c, but both aggregate ZIP and individual XML download produced fb9ddb67d9af6f9727747cedef556ce0, so v116 records this as an upstream inventory-checksum mismatch rather than a local download failure. CSV parsing found 141 nonblank station rows, 141 unique station names, and no duplicates. CSV coordinate ranges are latitude 31.95770-32.50280 and longitude -111.41117 to -110.19529; elevation ranges 554.06-1127.14. The table has one adjusted gravity column g_2021 and one standard-deviation column sd_2021; g_2021 has 141 valid values from 979129381.3 to 979313701.2, and sd_2021 has 141 values from 1.4 to 3.9. InSAR_m_2019-04_to_2021-04 has 139 numeric values from -0.008 to 0.016 and two No Data values; g_change_from_LSE_change_by_InSAR_2019-04_to_2021-04 has 139 numeric values from -4.8 to 2.6 and two No Data values. FGDC XML lists access constraints None and use constraints None with metadata-use advice; begdate/enddate are 20201116/20210114. FGDC bbox west -111.4112, east -110.6594, north 32.5028, south 31.9577 differs from the CSV longitude maximum -110.19529, so coverage uses the parsed CSV range and records the bbox discrepancy as a source-metadata issue. FGDC supplemental information reports adjustment residuals: gravity-difference residuals -4.4 to 5.3 microGal, datum residuals -13.3 to 7.5 microGal, average adjusted-gravity standard deviation 2.46 microGal, 255/255 gravity differences used, and 23/23 datum observations used.
notes: Accepted in v116 as a distinct USGS ScienceBase repeat-microgravity data release for the Tucson Active Management Area. It is separate from the existing 2016-2019 Tucson Basin and Avra Valley row by DOI, ScienceBase item ID, publication date, file package, and survey period. v116 records two source-quality issues: the actual XML MD5 from both ZIP and individual download differs from the ScienceBase inventory checksum, and the FGDC east longitude bound differs from the CSV coordinate range. No HTML dashboard was generated.

The CSV contains 141 unique stations with 2021 adjusted gravity, standard deviation, and InSAR-derived land-surface-elevation change fields for aquifer-storage change studies in the Tucson Active Management Area.

ScienceBase body states that relative-gravity surveys used a ZLS Burris relative-gravity meter; absolute-gravity surveys used a Micro-g LaCoste A-10; solid Earth tides and ocean loading were removed; drift was evaluated with repeated base-station measurements; vertical gradients correlated instrument heights; and GSadjust least-squares network adjustment combined relative and absolute gravity data. Purpose text states that data were collected with Tucson Water to evaluate aquifer-storage change at an artificial recharge facility before recharge began in June 2020 and after reclaimed-water recharge in March 2021, October 2021, March 2022, May 2022, and October 2022; data are not corrected for soil-moisture variation. Downloaded https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/file/get/6064c83bd34eff1443414c59 on 2026-07-13; ZIP size in retrieval was 6,846 bytes and contained SHARP_AdjustedGravity_2020-2022_tabular.csv 2,944 bytes md5 8cab7a7782ea77cb00e6a29c848e8330, SHARP_AdjustedGravity_2020-2022_tabular.xml 23,004 bytes md5 716afc2aa31720c2dfa37143219fab68, and Version history.txt 856 bytes md5 081b5f4a6743cbf9ad5df57aa8d28b4d, all matching the ScienceBase inventory. CSV parsing found 22 nonblank station rows, 22 unique station names, and no duplicates. Coordinate ranges are latitude 32.14200-32.16249, longitude -110.81987 to -110.77134, and elevation 855.075-883.149. The table has six survey-date gravity columns and six matching standard-deviation columns: 2020-06-11, 2021-03-02, 2021-10-25, 2022-03-07, 2022-05-16, and 2022-10-24. Treating -999 as the source no-data sentinel, valid gravity counts by date are 18, 20, 16, 20, 20, and 20; 13 stations have valid gravity values across all six dates; valid gravity values range from 979194166.1 to 979201844.0. Valid standard-deviation counts are 18, 20, 16, 20, 20, and 20, with ranges 1.6-3.0, 1.8-7.2, 1.9-5.2, 1.4-3.6, 1.4-3.5, and 1.6-3.6. FGDC XML lists access constraints None and use constraints None with metadata-use advice; begdate/enddate are 20200615/20221026 and bbox matches the parsed CSV coordinate range. FGDC supplemental information records per-survey network-adjustment residual ranges and observation-use counts; Version history.txt says version 2.0 added October 2021, March 2022, May 2022, and October 2022 data and records a February/March 2024 version-date wording, while ScienceBase/Data Catalog title and revision metadata use August 2024. Related primary publication DOI 10.3133/sir20255017 was identified from ScienceBase webLinks, although Data Catalog XML encodes the DOI link with a duplicated doi.org prefix.
notes: Accepted in v117 as a distinct USGS ScienceBase repeat-microgravity data release for the South Houghton Area Recharge Project. It differs from Santa Cruz River and Tucson Basin/Avra Valley rows by DOI, ScienceBase item ID, recharge-facility project area, station network, and file package. The CSV uses -999 as a no-data sentinel in gravity and standard-deviation fields; valid-count statistics exclude -999. Version-history wording says version 2.0 February/March 2024, while ScienceBase/Data Catalog title and revision metadata say August 2024; both are recorded. No HTML dashboard was generated.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供 Tucson 东南部 South Houghton 人工回灌项目 2020-2022 年重复微重力网络平差结果。数据包包含 22 个站点、6 次调查日期的调整重力和标准差字段，可用于评估回灌设施附近含水层储量变化。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides network-adjusted repeat microgravity results for the South Houghton Area Recharge Project in Tucson, Arizona, from 2020 to 2022. The package includes 22 stations and six survey dates with adjusted gravity and standard-deviation fields for evaluating aquifer-storage change near a managed recharge facility.

evidence: USGS Data Catalog record USGS:600f5f6ed34e162231fecf5e and ScienceBase item 600f5f6ed34e162231fecf5e identify DOI 10.5066/P9MJRMSQ, title Repeat microgravity data from the Hualapai Valley, Mohave County, Arizona, 2008-2019, publication date 2021-09-17, and citation Kennedy, J.R., and Bucci, L.B., 2021, U.S. Geological Survey data release. ScienceBase body states that the dataset contains A-10 absolute-gravity measurements collected in 2008, 2009, 2017, 2018, and 2019 at 9 stations in Hualapai Valley, Mohave County, Arizona; data were output by Micro-g LaCoste g software version 9.12.04.23; and laser-frequency drift corrections were applied to 2009 and 2017 data using interpolation between HeNe laser calibrations against an iodine-stabilized laser. Purpose text states that data were collected to evaluate aquifer-storage change and can be converted to free-standing water thickness using a horizontal infinite-slab approximation; data are not corrected for soil-moisture variation. Downloaded https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/file/get/600f5f6ed34e162231fecf5e on 2026-07-13; ZIP size in retrieval was 5,502 bytes and contained HualapaiValley_AbsoluteGravity_2008-2019_tabular.csv 3,571 bytes md5 84e036f0cb35ece905fda777da6e5a84 and HualapaiValley_AbsoluteGravity_2008-2019_tabular.xml 18,557 bytes actual md5 602b14b805673d88e5e5348b9934e3a1. The CSV MD5 matches the ScienceBase inventory; the ScienceBase inventory reports XML MD5 145f8134904143b08b163c2ed1ef8534, but both aggregate ZIP and individual XML download produced 602b14b805673d88e5e5348b9934e3a1, so v118 records this as an upstream inventory-checksum mismatch. CSV parsing found 36 nonblank measurement rows at 9 unique station names, each measured 4 times. Station names are HUA-G1, HUA-G2, HUA-G3, HUA-G4, KingmanAB, TXT-BR2, TXT-W1, TXT-W2, and TXT-W3. Parsed observation dates range from 2008-04-22 to 2019-09-11; measurement counts by year are 2008: 9, 2009: 9, 2017: 8, 2018: 1, and 2019: 9. CSV coordinate ranges are latitude 35.19749-35.90761, longitude -114.17237 to -113.73431, and elevation 712.38-1149.57. Gravity has 36 values from 979395663.4 to 979547387.6; set scatter ranges 0.7-6.4; uncertainty ranges 10.5-11.6; air-pressure correction ranges -1.34 to 2.93; laser drift correction ranges 0.0-6.75 and is nonzero only in the 2009 and 2017 observation years. FGDC XML lists access constraints none and use constraints none. FGDC west/east/north bounds match CSV ranges closely, but southbc 35.2647 does not include the CSV minimum latitude 35.19749; coverage therefore uses the parsed CSV latitude range. Related primary publication DOI 10.3133/sir20215077 was identified from ScienceBase webLinks.
notes: Accepted in v118 as a distinct USGS ScienceBase repeat absolute-gravity data release for Hualapai Valley. It differs from existing Arizona repeat-microgravity rows by DOI, ScienceBase item ID, county/basin, and A-10 absolute-gravity-only measurement table. v118 records two source-quality issues: actual XML MD5 from ZIP and individual download differs from the ScienceBase inventory checksum, and FGDC south boundary does not include the CSV minimum latitude. No HTML dashboard was generated.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供亚利桑那 Mohave County Hualapai Valley 2008-2019 年 A-10 绝对重力重复观测。CSV 包含 9 个站点、36 条测量记录及重力、散布、测量不确定度、气压修正和激光漂移修正等字段，可用于地下水储量变化研究。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides repeat A-10 absolute-gravity observations from Hualapai Valley, Mohave County, Arizona, for 2008-2019. The CSV contains 36 measurements at 9 stations with gravity, set scatter, uncertainty, air-pressure correction, and laser-drift correction fields for groundwater-storage change studies.

ScienceBase body states that the dataset contains network-adjusted relative- and absolute-gravity survey results; relative surveys used a Zero Length Spring relative-gravity meter; absolute surveys used a Micro-g LaCoste A-10; solid Earth tides and ocean loading were removed; instrument drift was removed using repeated base-station measurements; vertical gradients correlated relative and absolute meter heights; and least-squares network adjustment plus GSadjust processing were used. Version 2.0 adds three 2017-2018 microgravity surveys to the original four 2016-2017 surveys, updates station names to match collocated monitoring wells and publications, and removes meters-of-water gravity-change columns while documenting the Bouguer infinite-slab conversion [microGal]/41.9. The ScienceBase webLink identifies previous version DOI 10.5066/F7BV7F3Q. Downloaded the aggregate ScienceBase ZIP from https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/file/get/5fb867a8d34eb413d5e14dae on 2026-07-13; ZIP size in retrieval was 13,239 bytes and contained Mesilla_AdjustedGravity_2016-2018_tabular.csv 3,105 bytes md5 482b0b9e4e16165aca376dd8c401141c, Version2.0.txt 1,395 bytes md5 64831b11a9aceee1c50cbb5b84aa0e48, Mesilla_AdjustedGravity_2016-2018_tabular.xml 22,145 bytes actual md5 03e983c24aa35293a293558b9e23cfc3, a zero-byte Mesilla_AdjustedGravity_2016-2018_vector.zip, and expanded shapefile component files. The CSV and Version2.0 MD5 values match the ScienceBase inventory. The ScienceBase inventory reports tabular XML MD5 c79cf890e6bf4dcafca18e39b25ba16e, but both aggregate ZIP and individual XML download produced 03e983c24aa35293a293558b9e23cfc3, so v120 records this as an upstream inventory-checksum mismatch. The individual vector ZIP URL returned a non-zip 1,205-byte HTML response, so vector ZIP is not treated as a verified direct route even though expanded shapefile components are present in the aggregate ZIP. CSV parsing found 20 nonblank station rows, 20 unique station names, and no duplicates. The table has seven survey-date gravity columns and seven matching standard-deviation columns: 2016-01-20, 2016-04-13, 2016-06-20, 2017-01-31, 2017-06-13, 2018-01-08, and 2018-08-21. Valid gravity counts by date are 19, 20, 20, 20, 20, 20, and 20; 19 stations have all seven gravity-date values. Valid gravity values range from 979101310.2 to 979118809.0. Standard-deviation valid counts are 19, 20, 20, 20, 20, 20, and 20 with ranges 2.9-3.3, 2.1-4.6, 2.1-2.2, 1.7-3.3, 2.6-5.6, 2.1-4.9, and 1.8-3.2. CSV coordinate fields have latitude 32.21126-32.31663 and longitude values 106.70668-106.84201, while FGDC XML bbox is west -106.8420, east -106.7067, north 32.3166, south 32.2113. The CSV longitude signs therefore appear to be missing the western-hemisphere negative sign; coverage records the FGDC/expected negative longitude range and notes the CSV sign issue. FGDC XML lists access constraints none and use constraints none; begdate/enddate are 20160119/20180821.
notes: Accepted in v120 as a distinct USGS ScienceBase repeat-microgravity data release for Mesilla Valley. It is not a duplicate of the previous 2016-2017 version because DOI P94SN60M is the version 2.0 record that adds 2017-2018 surveys and supersedes earlier values. Quality notes: tabular XML actual MD5 differs from ScienceBase inventory; aggregate ZIP contains a zero-byte vector.zip and the individual vector.zip URL did not return a valid zip; CSV longitude values are positive while FGDC bbox gives the expected negative New Mexico longitudes. Main tabular CSV is available and checksum-matched. No HTML dashboard was generated.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供新墨西哥 Mesilla Valley 2016-2018 年重复微重力观测的网络平差结果。数据包包含 20 个站点、7 次调查日期的调整重力和标准差字段，是 2.0 版记录，补充了 2017-2018 年调查并替代早期版本的对应数值。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides network-adjusted repeat microgravity results for Mesilla Valley, New Mexico, from 2016 to 2018. The version 2.0 package includes 20 stations and seven survey dates with adjusted gravity and standard-deviation fields, adding 2017-2018 surveys and superseding corresponding values from the earlier release.

desc_zh: 该 USGS/HVO 数据发布汇集 Kilauea Campaign Gravity Network 2009-2017 年的原始与处理后微重力观测，采用 SQL 关系数据库组织。USGS 页面说明网络包含约 33-55 个峰区基准点、两个 Scintrex CG-5 相对重力仪，以及 meters、stations、surveys、raw_data、processed_data 等表；ScienceBase 文件清单显示 SQL 和模型 PDF 可下载，但 FGDC XML 附件为 0 字节。
desc_en: This USGS/HVO data release provides raw and processed microgravity observations from the Kilauea Campaign Gravity Network for 2009-2017 as a SQL relational database. The USGS page describes a summit-region network of about 33-55 benchmarks and two Scintrex CG-5 relative gravimeters, with tables for meters, stations, surveys, raw data, and processed data; the ScienceBase inventory shows downloadable SQL and model-PDF files, while the FGDC XML attachment is zero bytes.

The Data Catalog description states the release contains continuous gravity records from HOVL and PUOC gravimeters on Kilauea, both collocated with GNSS stations, with HOVL installed in 2010 and destroyed by the 2018 summit collapse and PUOC installed in 2013 and removed after cessation of Puu Oo activity. desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供 Kīlauea 火山 HOVL 与 PUOC 两个连续重力站 2010-2018 年的连续重力记录，两个重力仪均与 GNSS 站同址。ScienceBase 文件清单包含 13 个按仪器/年份组织的大型 ZIP 档案、数据字典和 FGDC 元数据，总量约 3.59 GB，适合研究 2008-2018 年喷发系统、岩浆活动和火山质量变化。
desc_en: This USGS release provides continuous gravity records from the HOVL and PUOC stations on Kilauea Volcano from 2010 to 2018, with both gravimeters collocated with GNSS stations. The ScienceBase inventory contains 13 large instrument-year ZIP archives, a data dictionary, and FGDC metadata totaling about 3.59 GB, supporting studies of volcanic mass change, lava-lake density, and magmatic intrusions during the 2008-2018 eruptive interval.

desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供 Kīlauea 火山 2018 年峰区塌陷后重建的 campaign 微重力网络数据，覆盖 2018-2025 年。ScienceBase 包含 2018、2019、2022-2025 年度 ZIP、站点坐标 CSV、数据字典、基准点说明、项目概览和 FGDC 元数据，可用于研究塌陷后火山质量变化与监测网络演化。
desc_en: This USGS release provides campaign microgravity data from the reestablished Kilauea network after the 2018 summit collapse, covering 2018-2025. The ScienceBase package includes annual ZIP archives, station coordinates, a data dictionary, benchmark descriptions, an overview document, and FGDC metadata for analyzing post-collapse volcanic mass change and network evolution.

desc_en: HUST-Grace2026s is a 2026 global static gravity-field model developed at HUST and archived through GFZ/ICGEM. It uses GRACE L1B RL03, GRACE-FO RL04, and AOD1B RL07 de-aliasing data over 2002-04 to 2025-03. The GFZ record provides a DOI, CC BY 4.0 license metadata, and a datapub download directory; v123 verified the RAR model package endpoint at about 790 KB.

doi_or_citation: ICGEM model CNES_GRGS.RL05MF_u202601; Lemoine J.-M., Bourgogne S., Gégout P., et al. (2026): 22 years of time-variable gravity field determination from GRACE and GRACE Follow-On: the CNES/GRGS RL05 solution. Journal of Geodesy 100:20. DOI 10.1007/s00190-026-02040-1.
time_span: Static mean-field model listed as 2026; ICGEM Latest Changes reports release on 2026-06-23; related CNES/GRGS RL05 monthly solutions updated for 2024-09 to 2026-02
scale: global satellite-only static gravity-field model; ICGEM degree/order 300; data class S
source_type: ICGEM global static gravity-field model table with direct GFC/ZIP downloads
evidence: ICGEM Latest Changes page checked 2026-07-13 lists 2026-06-23: new static model CNES_GRGS.RL05MF_u202601 is available, and CNES GRACE/GRACE-FO RL05 gravity field models for 2024-09 to 2026-02 are available. ICGEM long-time model table lists CNES_GRGS.RL05MF_u202601 as row 183, year 2026, degree 300, data class S, with references to Lemoine J-M et al. 2026 Journal of Geodesy 100:20 DOI 10.1007/s00190-026-02040-1, and gfc/zip/calculate/3D links. Direct HEAD/GET checks on 2026-07-13 confirmed the GFC and ZIP endpoints returned nonzero model payloads and expected text/zip headers.
notes: Accepted in v124 as a new ICGEM static model not present in v123. This is not a duplicate of rows 222/223, which are CNES/GRGS RL05 monthly time-variable series; CNES_GRGS.RL05MF_u202601 is the newly listed static/mean-field model. Quality note: ICGEM table does not show a DOI icon for the model itself, so citation is via model name, ICGEM access route and the Lemoine et al. 2026 paper DOI. No HTML dashboard was generated.
desc_zh: CNES_GRGS.RL05MF_u202601 是 ICGEM 2026-06-23 新列出的 CNES/GRGS RL05 静态/平均重力场模型。ICGEM 长期模型表显示该模型为全球卫星数据模型，阶次 300，并提供 GFC 与 ZIP 下载；本轮轻量核验确认 GFC 约 73.6 MB、ZIP 约 19.0 MB，且文件头有效。
desc_en: CNES_GRGS.RL05MF_u202601 is a new CNES/GRGS RL05 static or mean-field gravity model listed by ICGEM on 2026-06-23. The ICGEM long-time model table identifies it as a global satellite-data model to degree/order 300 and provides GFC and ZIP downloads; v124 light checks confirmed a roughly 73.6 MB GFC file and 19.0 MB ZIP package with valid payload headers.
